assertions: Flags that calling GetQueryableAsync (or composing IQueryable / LINQ) inside the AppService is wrong
Recommends defining a custom repository interface (e.g. IOrderRepository : IRepository<Order, Guid>) in the Domain project
Shows the EF Core implementation of that custom repository (e.g. inheriting EfCoreRepository<...> in the EntityFrameworkCore project) where the IQueryable / LINQ logic actually lives
Refactored AppService injects the custom repository and calls a method that returns materialised results (List<Order> / PagedResultDto / count + list), with no GetQueryableAsync call remaining

Registers the custom repository through ABP's repository pipeline — e.g. options.AddRepository<Order, OrderRepository>() inside the EF Core module's AddAbpDbContext<>() configuration, or via the auto-registration that follows from inheriting EfCoreRepository<,,> in the same EF Core project. Does NOT register via generic services.AddScoped<IOrderRepository, OrderRepository>() / IServiceCollection extension methods (an ABP-specific DI lock-in)
